title: poa angustifolia var. alpigena
intro: Poa angustifolia var. alpigena, also known as Alpine Bluegrass, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain due to its high concentration of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Poa angustifolia var. alpigena include its potential to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and ease digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
